 # HANDOFF — Remaining open items
 
 Open items that need continuation context are listed below.
 
 ---
+
+## RFQ SSRM migration — **IMPLEMENTED LOCALLY, NEEDS AUTHENTICATED SANDBOX VERIFICATION** (updated 2026-07-08)
+
+### 1. What this application is
+
+Designflow is POP Creations' product-lifecycle-management web application. The Angular frontend in
+`C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend` and Node/Express backend in
+`C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend` power RFQ, Item Library, production/licensing tracking, and
+related PLM workflows. RFQ is the business-critical quoting grid: sourcing and sales users manage RFQ
+items, vendor/factory quote rows, landed-cost math, sell prices, buyer targets, and margin calculations.
+The frontend runs as an Angular SPA; the backend runs on Cloud Run behind the BFF and talks to PostgreSQL
+through Sequelize.
+
+### 2. What we set out to do this session, and why
+
+Albert asked to sync the dflow repos and implement `docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`. The business goal is
+to stop the RFQ page from loading all 3000+ rows into the browser at once. The technical goal is an AG Grid
+Server-Side Row Model migration: backend pagination/filtering/sorting/grouping in SQL, while preserving the
+existing inline `Vendors[]` array that feeds the RFQ vendor detail grid. The plan required keeping the legacy
+`/getAllItem` endpoint as rollback and adding new paged/export endpoints.
+
+### 3. Current state
+
+All six dflow repos were synced on `sandbox-albert` before coding. Only these two repos now have work:
+
+- `designflow-backend`: new additive RFQ SSRM endpoints and query helper logic.
+- `designflow-frontend`: RFQ grid switched to `rowModelType: 'serverSide'`, new datasource, export endpoint
+  call, and carton dimension columns made non-sortable/non-filterable.
+
+Backend files changed:
+
+- `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`: added `RFQ_FIELD_MAP`, `buildBaseCondition`, filter/sort translators, Res
+  subqueries, merch-name-to-id filtering, group-level and leaf-level query builders. Leaf query uses
+  `subQuery: true` so `RFQVendors` has-many joins cannot truncate a row's inline vendor quote array.
+- `models/lib.model.js`: added `Customer.getAllPaged` and `Customer.exportAll`; both reuse
+  `utility.remapRFQItem()` so leaf rows keep the same `Vendors[]` shape as the legacy endpoint.
+- `controllers/main.controller.js`: added `findAllPaged` (`SYS-106`) and `exportAll` (`SYS-107`).
+- `routes/lib.router.js`: added `POST /getAllItemPaged` and `POST /getAllItemExport` with the same roles as
+  `POST /getAllItem`.
+- `tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`: added unit coverage for base conditions, vendor identity condition,
+  merch lookup filtering, Res subqueries, numeric/text/set filters, sort translation, and `subQuery: true`.
+- `docs/architecture.md`: documents `/getAllItemPaged` and `/getAllItemExport`.
+
+Frontend files changed:
+
+- `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts`: added `getAllItemPaged()` and `getAllItemExport()`.
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts`: grid options now use SSRM; `getAllRFQItem()` installs
+  `createRfqServerSideDatasource()` instead of loading all rows; datasource posts AG Grid request state to
+  `/getAllItemPaged`; per-page rows still run through `prepareRowData()` and `preloadCellNotes()`.
+- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts`: `onBtExport()` calls `/getAllItemExport` with current filter/sort
+  context before running Excel export.
+- `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`: carton length/width/height are now
+  `sortable:false, filter:false`.
+- `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.spec.ts`: updated tests to assert the carton SSRM
+  exception while keeping numeric comparator coverage for the real numeric columns.
+
+Verification passed locally:
+
+- Backend: `npm test -- --runInBand` in `designflow-backend` — 43 suites, 407 tests passed.
+- Frontend: `yarn test --runInBand` in `designflow-frontend` — 66 suites, 751 tests passed.
+- Frontend TypeScript: `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` — clean.
+- Local dev build: `node --max_old_space_size=8192 ./node_modules/@angular/cli/bin/ng serve --configuration sandbox --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4200` — compiled successfully.
+
+Not yet verified:
+
+- The RFQ grid was not visually verified with real data. Local browser navigation to
+  `http://127.0.0.1:4200/apps/rfq` redirected to `http://127.0.0.1:4200/login`; the in-app browser had no
+  authenticated Designflow session. No authenticated RFQ group expansion, filter, sort, detail grid, or export
+  manual check has been completed.
+- Work is not committed/pushed/deployed yet at the time this handoff section was written. Commit both repos after
+  final review if continuing from this exact state.
+
+### 4. Everything we tried that did NOT work
+
+The first sync command pushed `designflow-frontend` before pulling the newer remote `sandbox-albert`; Git rejected
+the push as non-fast-forward. The script then pulled `origin/sandbox-albert`, fast-forwarding local frontend from
+`099a53be` to `5656a785`. No local work existed yet, so nothing was lost.
+
+`yarn start --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4200` did not start the dev server directly because this repo's `start` script is
+`npm test && ng serve`. Since the full test suite had already run, the correct command was the direct Angular serve
+command listed above.
+
+The focused RFQ grid config spec initially failed after carton columns were made `sortable:false`. The old test
+hardcoded carton dimensions in the numeric-sortable list. That was wrong under the SSRM migration plan because carton
+dimensions are intentionally not server-sort/filterable. The fix removed carton dimensions from the numeric-comparator
+list and added a specific carton SSRM exception test.
+
+Local visual verification did not work because the local RFQ route redirected to login. No attempt was made to bypass
+authentication or inject credentials. This remains a real manual verification gate.
+
+### 5. Root causes and key findings
+
+The root performance issue is that legacy `Customer.getAll` returns all RFQ rows and the frontend uses AG Grid's
+client-side row model. That made filters and sorts work over all rows, but at the cost of a heavy unbounded backend
+query and a large browser payload.
+
+The key RFQ-specific risk is the vendor detail grid. RFQ master rows must keep a complete inline `Vendors[]` array.
+The new backend leaf query uses `subQuery: true` and still calls `utility.remapRFQItem()` so the `Vendors[]` contract
+is preserved for detail expansion, `Res`, duplicate-factory checks, requote highlighting, and detail-row height.
+
+Group-level filtering needs the same lookup joins as leaf rows. `buildGroupLevelQuery()` now includes the RFQ lookup
+joins with empty attributes so filters like Status, Step, Customer, RFQ Group, merch fields, and Res can compile
+without selecting non-grouped columns.
+
+The migration is additive. Legacy `POST /getAllItem` remains untouched as rollback. New endpoints are:
+`POST /getAllItemPaged` and `POST /getAllItemExport`.
+
+### 6. Exact next steps
+
+1. Commit backend and frontend changes with Albert's noreply author.
+   - You'll know it worked when both repos have new local commits on `sandbox-albert` and `git status --short` is
+     clean except for intentional handoff/docs files.
+
+2. Push both repos to `origin/sandbox-albert`.
+   - You'll know it worked when GitHub shows both branch heads at the new SHAs and no push is rejected.
+
+3. Confirm/update the existing frontend PR (`sandbox-albert` -> `develop`) and backend PR if needed.
+   - You'll know it worked when the PR checks run against the new frontend and backend SHAs.
+
+4. Watch CI for both repos.
+   - You'll know it worked when GitHub Actions are green for the pushed SHAs. Do not merge; dflow is Uma-reviewed.
+
+5. Deploy/verify on the authenticated sandbox.
+   - Open RFQ as an internal user.
+   - Confirm top-level RFQ groups load.
+   - Expand a group and confirm leaf rows load.
+   - Expand a leaf detail panel and confirm the vendor quote sub-grid contains the expected `Vendors[]` rows.
+   - Apply filters for Price Code, Status, Customer, Res, and Product Type/Material and confirm totals/rows come from
+     the backend, not only loaded cache.
+   - Sort a numeric price column and Res; confirm ordering spans the full filtered set.
+   - Confirm default Step filter still applies.
+   - Switch Division/status controls and confirm the datasource refreshes.
+   - Export with a filter applied and confirm the file contains the full filtered set, not just the visible page.
+
+6. If the sandbox reveals SQL errors, inspect backend logs for `SYS-106` or `SYS-107`.
+   - You'll know the issue is in the new SSRM path if legacy `/getAllItem` still works and only paged/export requests
+     fail.
+
+### 7. Constraints and gotchas in force
+
+- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; PR base is `develop`; never push dflow work to `main`; never self-merge.
+- Keep `/getAllItem` intact as rollback.
+- Do not lazy-load RFQ vendors per row. Leaf rows must carry full inline `Vendors[]`.
+- Keep `subQuery: true` on leaf pages because `RFQVendors` is a has-many join.
+- Carton length/width/height are intentionally not sort/filterable under SSRM.
+- Full-dataset select-all remains out of scope; SSRM selection is loaded-pages-oriented unless Albert requests a
+  separate full-matching-set bulk action design.
+- The local in-app browser cannot visually verify RFQ without login.
+
+### 8. Access and environment
+
+- Local parent folder: `C:\repos\dflow plm`
+- Backend repo: `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend`, branch `sandbox-albert`, remote
+  `https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend.git`
+- Frontend repo: `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`, branch `sandbox-albert`, remote
+  `https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend.git`
+- Local frontend dev URL used for attempted verification: `http://127.0.0.1:4200`
+- Secrets were not read or changed. Secrets remain in 1Password vault `vibe_coding`.
+
+### 9. Open questions and risks
+
+- The plan's open question about Res filter semantics is implemented as string matching on the current `"responded/requested"`
+  label, e.g. `"3/5"`, `"3/"`, or `"/5"` via `ILIKE`.
+- Group-level highlighted-item behavior has not been manually verified. The legacy endpoint bumps a matching leaf in a
+  single client-side array; SSRM group ordering is now server recency-based, so confirm whether the containing group
+  must jump to the top when `highlightedItem` is set.
+- Export fetches the full filtered leaf set from the backend before invoking AG Grid export. Manual sandbox validation
+  is needed to confirm the generated workbook includes the full fetched set under SSRM, not only loaded blocks.
+- Lookup sorting under `subQuery: true` is implemented with literal joined-column ordering and needs real DB validation.
+- Index presence on `RFQVendor.RFQitem_id_fk`, `RFQItem.rfqItem_rfq_group`, and `RFQItem.rfqItem_date_modified` was not
+  checked.
+
+Self-audit passed: this handoff explains the app, the session goal, exact current state, failed attempts, root causes,
+concrete next steps with verification gates, constraints, access/environment, and remaining risks for a fresh
+developer with no chat context.
+
+---
 
 ## RFQ Buyer Target edit gate fix — **COMMITTED & PUSHED, PR open** (updated 2026-07-07)
*** End Patch

+# HANDOFF — RFQ SSRM migration
+
+## RFQ SSRM migration — implemented locally, needs authenticated sandbox verification (updated 2026-07-08)
+
+### 1. What this application is
+
+Designflow Backend is the Node/Express REST API for POP Creations' Designflow PLM system. It backs the Angular
+frontend in `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`. The RFQ grid is the critical quoting workflow: it stores RFQ
+items, vendor/factory quote rows, landed-cost inputs, sell prices, buyer targets, and margin data. This backend runs
+on Cloud Run behind the BFF and uses Sequelize against PostgreSQL.
+
+### 2. What we set out to do this session, and why
+
+Albert asked to sync the dflow repos and implement `designflow-frontend/docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`. The goal was
+to move RFQ list loading from an all-rows client-side grid to AG Grid Server-Side Row Model while keeping the legacy
+`/getAllItem` endpoint as rollback. The backend part is to add paged/grouped/filterable/sortable endpoints that still
+return the same remapped RFQ leaf rows, especially the inline `Vendors[]` array used by the frontend detail grid.
+
+### 3. Current state
+
+All six repos were synced on `sandbox-albert` before coding. Backend changes are local in this repo and not yet
+deployed at the time this handoff was written.
+
+Changed backend files:
+
+- `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`: added `RFQ_FIELD_MAP`, `buildBaseCondition`, `translateFilterModel`,
+  `translateSortModel`, `buildResSubqueries`, `buildMerchNameToIds`, `buildGroupLevelQuery`, and
+  `buildLeafLevelQuery`.
+- `models/lib.model.js`: added `Customer.getAllPaged` and `Customer.exportAll`. Both reuse
+  `utility.remapRFQItem()` for RFQ leaf rows so `Vendors[]` stays in the legacy shape.
+- `controllers/main.controller.js`: added `findAllPaged` (`SYS-106`) and `exportAll` (`SYS-107`).
+- `routes/lib.router.js`: added `POST /getAllItemPaged` and `POST /getAllItemExport` with the same role gate as
+  `POST /getAllItem`.
+- `tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`: added unit tests for base conditions, vendor branch, merch filters, Res
+  subqueries, filter/sort translators, and the `subQuery: true` leaf-page guard.
+- `docs/architecture.md`: documented the two new RFQ endpoints.
+
+Verified locally:
+
+- `npm test -- --runInBand` passed: 43 suites, 407 tests.
+
+Related frontend changes live in `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend` and are documented in that repo's
+`HANDOFF.md`.
+
+### 4. Everything we tried that did NOT work
+
+The frontend local visual check could not reach RFQ because `http://127.0.0.1:4200/apps/rfq` redirected to
+`/login` in the in-app browser. No authenticated local RFQ grid verification was completed.
+
+The first frontend sync push was rejected because the remote `sandbox-albert` had advanced. The repo was then pulled
+and fast-forwarded before any local code changes existed, so no work was lost.
+
+The frontend `yarn start` script ran `npm test` before serving. Since the full test suite had already run, the direct
+Angular serve command was used instead.
+
+### 5. Root causes and key findings
+
+Legacy `Customer.getAll` is an unbounded list query returning all RFQ rows. The new path keeps it untouched and adds
+`Customer.getAllPaged` / `Customer.exportAll`.
+
+The highest-risk backend detail is the `RFQVendors` has-many include. Leaf pages use `subQuery: true`; without that,
+`LIMIT/OFFSET` can paginate the flattened RFQItem-by-RFQVendor join and truncate a leaf row's `Vendors[]`.
+
+Group-level filtering must join the same lookup associations as leaf filtering. `buildGroupLevelQuery()` strips
+include attributes to avoid grouped-select problems but keeps the aliases available for translated filters.
+
+`Res` is implemented with correlated scalar subqueries and string matching on the current `"responded/requested"`
+label.
+
+### 6. Exact next steps
+
+1. Commit this backend repo with Albert's noreply author.
+   - You'll know it worked when `git log -1` shows the new SSRM backend commit on `sandbox-albert`.

+2. Push `sandbox-albert`.

+3. Watch backend CI.

+
+4. Deploy/verify the backend with the matching frontend branch on sandbox.
+   - You'll know it worked when authenticated RFQ page requests hit `/getAllItemPaged` and return `{ rows, lastRow }`.
+
+5. Check backend logs during manual RFQ testing.
+   - `SYS-106` means `/getAllItemPaged` failed.
+   - `SYS-107` means `/getAllItemExport` failed.
+   - Legacy `/getAllItem` should remain available as rollback.
+
+6. Validate real DB SQL behavior:
+   - Filter Status, Step, Customer, Res, Product Type/Material.
+   - Sort numeric price fields and Res.
+   - Expand a group and verify leaf rows include complete `Vendors[]`.
+   - Export with filters and verify the result contains the full filtered set.
+
+### 7. Constraints and gotchas in force
+
+- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; PR base is `develop`; never push dflow work to `main`; never self-merge.
+- Do not remove or change `/getAllItem`.
+- Do not lazy-load vendors per RFQ row.
+- Keep leaf query `subQuery: true`.
+- Keep `utility.remapRFQItem()` as the row-shaping source for RFQ leaf rows.
+- Carton dimensions are intentionally not server-sort/filterable.
+- Secrets live in 1Password vault `vibe_coding`; no secrets were read or changed.
+
+### 8. Access and environment

+
+### 9. Open questions and risks
+

+- Group-level highlighted-item behavior may need product confirmation: should the containing group jump to the top,
+  or is leaf-level highlighting inside the normal group order enough?
+- Index presence was not checked for `RFQVendor.RFQitem_id_fk`, `RFQItem.rfqItem_rfq_group`, or
+  `RFQItem.rfqItem_date_modified`.
+- Export backend returns the full filtered leaf set, but frontend workbook generation still needs authenticated

+

# HANDOFF — RFQ SSRM migration

## RFQ SSRM migration — implemented locally, needs authenticated sandbox verification (updated 2026-07-08)

### 1. What this application is

Designflow Backend is the Node/Express REST API for POP Creations' Designflow PLM system. It backs the Angular
frontend in `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`. The RFQ grid is the critical quoting workflow: it stores RFQ
items, vendor/factory quote rows, landed-cost inputs, sell prices, buyer targets, and margin data. This backend runs
on Cloud Run behind the BFF and uses Sequelize against PostgreSQL.

### 2. What we set out to do this session, and why

Albert asked to sync the dflow repos and implement `designflow-frontend/docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`. The goal was
to move RFQ list loading from an all-rows client-side grid to AG Grid Server-Side Row Model while keeping the legacy
`/getAllItem` endpoint as rollback. The backend part is to add paged/grouped/filterable/sortable endpoints that still
return the same remapped RFQ leaf rows, especially the inline `Vendors[]` array used by the frontend detail grid.

### 3. Current state

All six repos were synced on `sandbox-albert` before coding. Backend changes are local in this repo and not yet
deployed at the time this handoff was written.

Changed backend files:

- `helpers/rfqListQuery.js`: added `RFQ_FIELD_MAP`, `buildBaseCondition`, `translateFilterModel`,
  `translateSortModel`, `buildResSubqueries`, `buildMerchNameToIds`, `buildGroupLevelQuery`, and
  `buildLeafLevelQuery`.
- `models/lib.model.js`: added `Customer.getAllPaged` and `Customer.exportAll`. Both reuse
  `utility.remapRFQItem()` for RFQ leaf rows so `Vendors[]` stays in the legacy shape.
- `controllers/main.controller.js`: added `findAllPaged` (`SYS-106`) and `exportAll` (`SYS-107`).
- `routes/lib.router.js`: added `POST /getAllItemPaged` and `POST /getAllItemExport` with the same role gate as
  `POST /getAllItem`.
- `tests/unit/rfqListQuery.test.js`: added unit tests for base conditions, vendor branch, merch filters, Res
  subqueries, filter/sort translators, and the `subQuery: true` leaf-page guard.
- `docs/architecture.md`: documented the two new RFQ endpoints.

Verified locally:

- `npm test -- --runInBand` passed: 43 suites, 407 tests.

Related frontend changes live in `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend` and are documented in that repo's
`HANDOFF.md`.

### 4. Everything we tried that did NOT work

The frontend local visual check could not reach RFQ because `http://127.0.0.1:4200/apps/rfq` redirected to
`/login` in the in-app browser. No authenticated local RFQ grid verification was completed.

The first frontend sync push was rejected because the remote `sandbox-albert` had advanced. The repo was then pulled
and fast-forwarded before any local code changes existed, so no work was lost.

The frontend `yarn start` script ran `npm test` before serving. Since the full test suite had already run, the direct
Angular serve command was used instead.

### 5. Root causes and key findings

Legacy `Customer.getAll` is an unbounded list query returning all RFQ rows. The new path keeps it untouched and adds
`Customer.getAllPaged` / `Customer.exportAll`.

The highest-risk backend detail is the `RFQVendors` has-many include. Leaf pages use `subQuery: true`; without that,
`LIMIT/OFFSET` can paginate the flattened RFQItem-by-RFQVendor join and truncate a leaf row's `Vendors[]`.

Group-level filtering must join the same lookup associations as leaf filtering. `buildGroupLevelQuery()` strips
include attributes to avoid grouped-select problems but keeps the aliases available for translated filters.

`Res` is implemented with correlated scalar subqueries and string matching on the current `"responded/requested"`
label.

### 6. Exact next steps

1. Commit this backend repo with Albert's noreply author.
   - You'll know it worked when `git log -1` shows the new SSRM backend commit on `sandbox-albert`.

2. Push `sandbox-albert`.
   - You'll know it worked when GitHub shows the backend branch at the new SHA.

3. Watch backend CI.
   - You'll know it worked when all GitHub checks pass for the pushed SHA.

4. Deploy/verify the backend with the matching frontend branch on sandbox.
   - You'll know it worked when authenticated RFQ page requests hit `/getAllItemPaged` and return `{ rows, lastRow }`.

5. Check backend logs during manual RFQ testing.
   - `SYS-106` means `/getAllItemPaged` failed.
   - `SYS-107` means `/getAllItemExport` failed.
   - Legacy `/getAllItem` should remain available as rollback.

6. Validate real DB SQL behavior:
   - Filter Status, Step, Customer, Res, Product Type/Material.
   - Sort numeric price fields and Res.
   - Expand a group and verify leaf rows include complete `Vendors[]`.
   - Export with filters and verify the result contains the full filtered set.

### 7. Constraints and gotchas in force

- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; PR base is `develop`; never push dflow work to `main`; never self-merge.
- Do not remove or change `/getAllItem`.
- Do not lazy-load vendors per RFQ row.
- Keep leaf query `subQuery: true`.
- Keep `utility.remapRFQItem()` as the row-shaping source for RFQ leaf rows.
- Carton dimensions are intentionally not server-sort/filterable.
- Secrets live in 1Password vault `vibe_coding`; no secrets were read or changed.

### 8. Access and environment

- Repo: `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend`
- Branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Remote: `https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend.git`
- Frontend repo for paired changes: `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend`
- Authenticated CLIs were not needed beyond git for this local implementation.

### 9. Open questions and risks

- Real DB validation is still needed for lookup sorting under `subQuery: true`.
- Group-level highlighted-item behavior may need product confirmation: should the containing group jump to the top,
  or is leaf-level highlighting inside the normal group order enough?
- Index presence was not checked for `RFQVendor.RFQitem_id_fk`, `RFQItem.rfqItem_rfq_group`, or
  `RFQItem.rfqItem_date_modified`.
- Export backend returns the full filtered leaf set, but frontend workbook generation still needs authenticated
  browser validation.

Self-audit passed: this handoff explains the app, session goal, current state, failed attempts, root causes, exact
next steps with verification gates, constraints, access/environment, and open risks for a fresh developer with no
chat context.

The BFF attaches a Google Cloud OIDC token to every backend request so Cloud Run's infrastructure layer will accept the call (this is separate from user auth — it is Google Cloud service-to-service authentication). That Google OIDC token had the wrong `audience` claim because the BFF's `CORE_BACKEND_URL` was set to the custom domain `https://api.core.alsand.designflow.app`. Cloud Run validates OIDC token audiences against the service's `.run.app` URL only — custom domain URLs do not work as OIDC audiences, even when they are correctly mapped to the service via Cloud Run domain mappings. docs\deployment.md:243:gcloud run services update popcre-albert-bff-sandbox --region=us-central1 --project=lithe-breaker-323913 \ docs\deployment.md:244: --update-env-vars "CORE_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-albert-backend-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app,ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-albert-item-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app,TRACKING_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-albert-tracking-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app,DATA_SYNC_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-albert-sync-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app" docs\deployment.md:246:gcloud run services update popcre-bff-sandbox --region=us-central1 --project=lithe-breaker-323913 \ docs\deployment.md:247: --update-env-vars "CORE_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-core-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app,ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-item-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app,TRACKING_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-tracking-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app,DATA_SYNC_BACKEND_URL=https://popcre-sync-sandbox-mi7si7t62a-uc.a.run.app" docs\deployment.md:253:The BFF env files (`designflow-bff/env/.env.*`) were also updated to match. docs\deployment.md:255:**Rule:** `*_BACKEND_URL` values in the BFF must always be `.run.app` service URLs, never custom domains. payload.message:
Implemented and deployed the RFQ SSRM migration on `sandbox-albert`.

Backend:
- Commit: `aa74070eddd90d4958c7ae4ee064b20fe418549c`
- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/pull/60
- Added `/getAllItemPaged` and `/getAllItemExport`; legacy `/getAllItem` remains intact.
- Cloud Run verified: `popcre-albert-core-sandbox` has 100% traffic on image tagged with `aa74070...`.

Frontend:
- Commit: `960c01dd57812c29e7409a030dbd9399d6e55ab2`
- PR: https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/141
- RFQ grid now uses AG Grid SSRM datasource; export calls the new full filtered-set export endpoint.
- Cloud Run verified: `popcre-albert-frontend-sandbox` has 100% traffic on image tagged with `960c01dd...`.

Verification passed:
- Backend: `npm test -- --runInBand` — 43 suites / 407 tests.
- Frontend: `yarn test --runInBand` — 66 suites / 751 tests.
- Frontend TypeScript: `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit`.
- Both PR heads: GitHub check green + Cloud Build green.
- Handoffs updated in both repos.

One remaining gap: I could not visually verify the RFQ grid itself because local `/apps/rfq` redirected to `/login` in the in-app browser. The deployed sandbox still needs an authenticated RFQ smoke test: load groups, expand rows, confirm vendor detail grid, filter/sort across full set, and export filtered results.

Backend/frontend working trees are clean. Other sibling repos now show unrelated local changes; I did not touch them.
